notes: Runs out of the box. High settings 30-40 fps. Sometimes there  is a little stuttering - lag - in the open world which I don't have in Windows 10.
title: ELEX

notes: The game runs fine with DXVK in 4k on Plasma Wayland. Framerate is ok, but it stutters from time to time. But playable! To exit the game crashes my machine and I hat to reset.
title: ELEX

notes: None of the tweaks work for me. The Game starts and i can load a savegame, but ingame there are massive graphic bugs.
title: ELEX

notes: Game runs out of the box without an issue. At first I got the black screen crash after the Piranha logo, after I restarted my computer it worked fine. With VSync on there were fps drops. Disabled it and the performance is much better. I don't have a high end pc and I am getting around 70 fps on high settings. No glitches so far.
title: ELEX

notes: Unplug all controllers or it will crash after the developer's logo. If you have issues with keyboard input remove xboxdrv from your system.
title: ELEX

notes:
xbox controller plugged in, game won't start and hung. From proton github page, it is a known issue for some peripherals. Unplugging Controller and game will start.
Another issue which i could not find a solution, is that game only recognize monitor as 60Hz, leading to stuttering when and if you manage to push the game above that (for example during cut scenes). Steam fps limiter does not work. Another small issue, being sounds not always at balanced volume (some voices heard less than music). But all aside, these issues are indeed minor, and game is perfectly playable and fine nonetheless.
title: ELEX

notes:
Had to disable Ambient Occlusion, otherwise the game would only have like 5 fps.
Changing the Shader Quality led to a freeze. Otherwise playing the Game on high settings and getting 30 fps.
title: ELEX

notes: When you load game, you have some drops FPS, but after +- 2 minutes, its ok :)

title: ELEX

notes: The game started just fine. There were no big problems with changing graphics settings. When the game gets stuck on changing shader settings it was enough to tab out and back in again. The Problem: After watching the intro the game crashes when displaying particle effects. (Mainly when the plane gets hit.) Solution: I patched proton 4.2-2 manually with the current (v1.0.2) version of DXVK. It was not enough to just use the install script on the wine prefix for Elex that proton generated. I also had to change the ".dll" files for DXVK in the proton main directory.
title: ELEX

notes: I have played this game from start to finish on linux with no problems.
title: ELEX

notes: I tested whole campaign and side quests. With AMDVLK 2019.Q2.4 driver I have the same performance on Ubuntu 19.04 like on Windows 10 (I compared fps on both systems in few places, settings: 1920x1080 + ultra). With RADV driver a performance is 10% worse.
title: ELEX

notes: There are some extremly rare slowdowns/stutters on entering new areas. Beside them the game works like a charm.
title: ELEX

notes: First I tried with Proton 4.2, but this gave me game freezes with particle effects (jetpack and torches). Switching to version 3.7-8 resolved the freezes. Performance is excellent.
title: ELEX

notes: When using nvidia and proton 4.2 the game crashes when using the jetpack. Make a dxvk.conf in the elex directory and put dxgi.deferSurfaceCreation = True in there. Note that nobody knows why this workaround works.
title: ELEX

notes: #### Terminal=  sudo systemctl disable xboxdrv ##and## systemctl stop xboxdrv #### If you have Games who need xboxdrv try to add #### Launch Options other games= systemctl start xboxdrv && %command% ##for## Launch Options ELEX= systemctl stop xboxdrv && %command% #### Runs Perfect
title: ELEX

notes:
It ran pretty much perfectly since Proton 4.11-1 (Had crashes using the Jetpack in Proton 4.2.9)
Played it already for 30h+ and had not a single Issue!
[Small Disclosure: I've used the GOG-Version, imported into Steam with Proton manually enabled]
title: ELEX

notes: Played 14 hours with mouse and keyboard so far. Runs perfectly out of the box. Performance isn't great, but acceptable for a RPG (35-60 fps). Graphic settings change nothing about fps, so I assume it's because of my old FX CPU.
